---
title: "Molecules of venomous animals"
---

[![Alpha-bungarotoxins are found in the venom of snakes of the genus Bungarus.](alfabungarotoxina.png){width="40%"}](https://chemapps.stolaf.edu/jmol/jmol.php?&pdbid=1IK8)

<p style="text-align:right;">

[Wikipedia](https://gl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bungarotoxin)

</p>

[![Hyaluronidase is an enzyme that facilitates the dissemination of venom in tissues, present in snake venoms and spiders.](hialuronidase.png){width="40%"}](https://chemapps.stolaf.edu/jmol/jmol.php?model=%20CC1(C(=C)N(C2=CC=CC=C21)CCCCCC(=O)O)C.Br)

<p style="text-align:right;">

[Wikipedia](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyaluronidase)

</p>

[![Conotoxins, conotoxins are a group of neurotoxic peptides, isolated from the venom of marine snails of the genus Conus.](conotoxina.png){width="40%"}](https://chemapps.stolaf.edu/jmol/jmol.php?&pdbid=1CNL)

<p style="text-align:right;">

[Wikipedia](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conotoxin)

</p>
